# 📝 Notes

May 6, 2026

## 8am AI\!

Invited [David Olsson](mailto:david@atomic47.co) [eric@scroll.ca](mailto:eric@scroll.ca) [giroday@gmail.com](mailto:giroday@gmail.com) [hanif.janmohamed@gmail.com](mailto:hanif.janmohamed@gmail.com) [Fulvio Ciano](mailto:fulvio@atomic47.co) [scott@driftresourcetechnologies.com](mailto:scott@driftresourcetechnologies.com) [Jason Kryski](mailto:jason@mttr.ca) [Jen Boger](mailto:jen@atomic47.co) [Shabraj Shahid](mailto:shabraj@mttr.ca) [spencer@ratiomachina.com](mailto:spencer@ratiomachina.com) [graham@ratiomachina.com](mailto:graham@ratiomachina.com) [Bailey Klinger](mailto:bwklinger@gmail.com) [andre.bertram3@gmail.com](mailto:andre.bertram3@gmail.com) [Spencer MacBeth](mailto:spencer@mttr.ca) [mike@smartdeskcrm.com](mailto:mike@smartdeskcrm.com)

Attachments [8am AI\!](https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?eid=aW52N2w4MWJvNjJqYm5zYmQxZTMzYjllNmZfMjAyNjA1MDZUMTUwMDAwWiBkYXZpZEBhdG9taWM0Ny5jbw)

Meeting records [Transcript](https://docs.google.com/document/d/14h1cnFnN60SzLep8CYCJ6n1IRX9QEhD9Of5toLkVqyo/edit?usp=drive_web&tab=t.1ra2nivlw4k) 

### Summary

Meeting discussions centered on Project State architecture for capturing organizational intelligence via local file system structures.

**Project State System Architecture**  
Project State creates a centralized intelligence layer by harvesting activity signals from distributed environments into local flat files. This system enables queryable project history and context management directly within development environments.

**LLM Integration and Value**  
The system leverages large language models to structure unstructured data and provide autonomous task management or critique. Project State ultimately functions as a persistent cognitive container for complex organizational workflows.

**Implementation and Future Strategy**  
Development priorities include publishing a foundational white paper and improving accessibility for small teams. The primary decision was to utilize flat file models like YAML to ensure vendor portability for organizational knowledge.

*Rate this Summary:* [Helpful](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_4YkxrBAaiTVqYCi?isGoogler=false&isHelpful=true) or [Not Helpful](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_4YkxrBAaiTVqYCi?isGoogler=false&isHelpful=false)

### Next steps

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Attract Companies: Attract companies for testing; build required installers for usability validation.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Share Assets: Make project assets accessible to stakeholders.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Send Followup: Send meeting followup email to attendees.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Onboard Testers: Onboard individuals or small teams to test project state functionality.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Design Server: Design server level instance allowing autonomous cloud operation.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Expose Pattern: Share the Project State pattern in its raw format with the group.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Ensure Portability: Work on making the Project State solution portable for sharing across other organizations.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Solicit Organizations: Put out a call to the group seeking organizations willing to test the Project State system.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Schedule Meeting: Schedule a meeting with Hanif Janmohamed to discuss real world AI implementation requirements.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Share Document: Share built document regarding Crush Dynamics fermentation technology.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Project State: Make Project State operation mode known for knowledge workers.

- [ ] \[Hanif Janmohamed, Eric, a few others\] Review Whitepaper: Review David Olssons white paper before submission to Arvix.

### Details

*Did the screenshots in this section make your notes [better](https://goo.gle/44IH7J9) or [worse](https://goo.gle/4b5ZWtJ)?*

* **Introduction and Setup**: David Olsson initiated the meeting and worked on setting up their environment, noting the manual steps still required rather than relying on voice commands alone. They successfully ran a script named \`CD count conbon npm run\` and mentioned having skills set up to trigger these types of commands, indicating they planned to demonstrate work they believe is broadly valuable ([00:00:05](#00:00:05)).

* **Introduction to Project State**: David Olsson introduced "Project State," a tool designed to maintain a comprehensive memory of a project's status at any given time. The tool primarily focuses on providing "intelligence" around a project by storing information based on a specific schema, which is then utilized by large language models, typically within an Integrated Development Environment (IDE) ([00:01:46](#00:01:46)).

  ![][image1]

* **Project State's Four Layers and Signal Harvesting**: The system is conceptualized as four (possibly five or one) layers, with a foundational component called "Work State" that harvests activity signals from various connectors such as GitHub, Gmail, Slack, Jira, Confluence, and RSS feeds. This harvesting of "signals" addresses the challenge of distributed work across numerous environments and repositories, and David Olsson stated they would demonstrate a Kanban view of this Work State ([00:02:50](#00:02:50)).

  ![][image2]

* **Origin and Purpose of Project State**: Project State was initially designed to manage the Crush Dynamics project, which involved protein generation or fermentation and had complex reporting needs across three companies and four organizations ([00:02:50](#00:02:50)). The need for a centralized "intelligence" source was crucial to efficiently handle specific, long, and expensive reporting formats required by different organizations ([00:03:57](#00:03:57)).

* **Data Model and Project Portfolios**: The system is built upon a flat file model, operating at the metadata level, which was the original architectural approach for Work Sona ([00:03:57](#00:03:57)). David Olsson noted the high volume of projects and the necessity of viewing them in "portfolios" or groups, which led to the creation of a comprehensive view that tracks all work by harvesting signals at a granular level ([00:05:01](#00:05:01)).

  ![][image3]

* **Value and Strategic Application**: The core value of the system lies in its ability to track all work and communication around a project from various sources, making the intelligence queryable from within the IDE using an LLM ([00:05:01](#00:05:01)). The layering of intelligence—from doing the work, collecting signals, managing at the project level, and then scaling up to a "docket level" for the business—is formalized in the "Strategic State" component ([00:06:06](#00:06:06)).

  ![][image4]

* **Data Structure and Automated Reporting**: Project State uses files (like YAML, Markdown, JSON) stored locally to document information such as changes, communication, decisions, milestones, and phases ([00:07:06](#00:07:06)) ([00:40:19](#00:40:19)). The system generates weekly reports, including scripted emails on Friday afternoons and retrospective reports, and can deliver these documents in traditional formats, such as doc files and spreadsheets, to accommodate organizations with "hexile-heavy", template-based document requirements ([00:08:35](#00:08:35)).

  ![][image5]

* **Intelligence Storage and LLM Usage**: A significant revelation was the use of YAML files for data storage, which offers greater value for AI consumption and organizational data capture. The design ensures that intelligence is not exclusively captured within the LLM itself, which keeps the data within the project context and aligns with the flat file model ([00:03:57](#00:03:57)) ([00:10:12](#00:10:12)).

* **Strategic State and Customer Profiles**: Strategic State enables working at a higher organizational level and has facilitated the creation of high-quality customer and company profiles that can be integrated into a Customer Relationship Management (CRM) experience. This capability allows for the creation of up-to-date "battle cards" and profiles that detail engagement strategies and value propositions ([00:07:06](#00:07:06)) ([00:13:01](#00:13:01)).

  ![][image6]

* **Efficiency and Autonomous Work**: David Olsson found that the new system, despite the extensive development, has actually allowed them to slow down and avoid the feeling of being overwhelmed by work. The system's ability to create layered intelligence using various tools, along with the accessibility of working within the chat experience, suggests a large opportunity for accessible intelligence without a complex UI ([00:14:27](#00:14:27)).

* **Unstructured Data and Cost Management**: The system can process and find continuity in highly unstructured data, such as 15 pages of daily handwritten notes, provided the data is collected longitudinally over time. Working inside the IDE with cloud code makes the system's token usage accessible, as running the entire process through API tokens would be prohibitively expensive ([00:15:47](#00:15:47)).

* **Implementation of "Packs" and Pattern Structure**: The architecture includes "packs," which are pre-configured tracking setups that can be invoked for specific tasks, such as tracking time or sales lead generation ([00:18:06](#00:18:06)). David Olsson emphasized that the "structure as intelligence" concept has been fully realized, allowing the system to establish what it needs to track based on user inputs, questions, or anxieties ([00:16:53](#00:16:53)).

* **System Accessibility and Future Plans**: David Olsson plans to make the system accessible to individuals and small teams, offering onboarding and testing opportunities. They noted that the pattern is a powerful one for automating infrastructure in a cognitive way and that they plan to design a server-level, autonomous cloud instance of the system ([00:21:03](#00:21:03)).

* **Shared Experience of Non-Stop Ideas**: Mike Grell resonated with the excitement and lack of sleep experienced by David Olsson, noting that their mind is constantly generating ideas because the feeling of "if you can dream it, you can do it" is now a reality due to rapid AI advancements ([00:23:46](#00:23:46)). David Olsson agreed that AI allows for 100x improvement in their discipline and that building robust apps is becoming dramatically faster and cheaper ([00:24:51](#00:24:51)).

* **LLM Competency and AGI Discussion**: David Olsson noted the increasing competency of models, citing the availability of a 12 million token context window, which removes previous limits ([00:25:45](#00:25:45)). They expressed the feeling that the current state of technology, particularly with models like Opus 4.7, approaches General Intelligence (AGI) that is capable of managing the operational side of a business by surfacing signal gaps ([00:26:56](#00:26:56)).

* **Cloud Code Desktop Interface and Autonomous Functionality**: Mike Grell inquired about the differences between using the command line interface and the desktop app for cloud code ([00:28:04](#00:28:04)). David Olsson confirmed that the desktop app offers more autonomous functionality and that they recommend using it for skills development, though they noted that the global and local accessibility of plugins within the ecosystem can still be complex ([00:29:15](#00:29:15)).

* **Importance of Commits and Autonomous Task Management**: Mike Grell shared an experience of losing experimental work due to forgetting to commit, reinforcing the importance of frequent commits ([00:30:06](#00:30:06)). David Olsson suggested automating commits within the IDE rules and noted that the task-level execution is highly effective, with the desktop app marshalling parallel agents and performing complex orchestration ([00:30:59](#00:30:59)) ([00:33:05](#00:33:05)).

* **Agentic Systems and Vendor Portability**: David Olsson noted that most substrates now deal with skills, allowing for the portability of "memories" and data ingestion from other vendors ([00:34:08](#00:34:08)). The agentic plane is well-understood, and the data is stored in flat files (YAML, Markdown, JSON) to ensure the knowledge is portable and not vendor-aligned ([00:35:05](#00:35:05)) ([00:40:19](#00:40:19)).

* **Alternative Tools (Codex) and Harness Importance**: Jason Kryski, needing to leave, suggested Mike Grell try out Codex desktop and GPT-5 for comparison, noting that some users are getting better outputs from the latter ([00:35:05](#00:35:05)). Jason Kryski emphasized that the "harness"—the system wrapping the model—is a crucial component that dictates how intelligent the experience feels, aligning with David Olsson's Project State structure ([00:37:10](#00:37:10)).

* **Organizational Use Cases for File System Indexing**: David Olsson mentioned that the system's flat file approach is highly powerful at the file system level and is effective for companies with large, unorganized collections of files and spreadsheets ([00:39:15](#00:39:15)). The Project State pattern can be quickly used to index a company's SharePoint to provide a functional and valued understanding of their knowledge ([00:40:19](#00:40:19)).

* **Call for Collaboration on Manufacturer Support**: Bailey Klinger, arriving late, requested volunteers to provide casual office hours for members of the newly formed Manufacturers Association in the Okonogan. These members, often small two-to-five-person manufacturers, need impartial guidance on tech adoption, productivity, and the AI transition, as their primary resource for this information is often biased vendors ([00:42:22](#00:42:22)).

* **Reflecting on Personal Learning and the LLM's Role**: David Olsson reflected on the benefit of having their automated system critique their work and surface their "flaws," finding it valuable for learning and quality control ([00:45:45](#00:45:45)). Juan shared a similar experience where the LLM (Opus) initially annoyed them by pushing back, but they eventually tuned the model's communication style to constructively challenge them and expose blind spots and logic gaps ([00:47:47](#00:47:47)).

* **The Value of LLM Context and System Learning**: Juan explained that they trained their agent to increase context and provide pushback only when analyzing problems from a system or logic point of view, which has positively influenced their work ([00:49:06](#00:49:06)) ([00:51:09](#00:51:09)). David Olsson agreed that this ability to listen differently and ask "why" the model made a statement is essential for learning and tuning, especially when moving into new areas of computing science ([00:52:19](#00:52:19)).

* **Project State's Function as Context Carrier**: David Olsson reiterated that the core purpose of Project State is to eliminate the need for individuals to "carry" all their cognitive learnings and revelations, allowing the state to capture and analyze them across different schemas ([00:53:43](#00:53:43)). Hanif Janmohamed found the metaphor of Project State as "what you carry in your head" to be a helpful way to understand the system ([00:55:05](#00:55:05)).

* **Project Initiation and Prompting**: When initiating a new project, David Olsson inputs a set of "seminal" or influential documents, conversations, and desired outcomes (including unknowns) ([00:56:08](#00:56:08)). With only a few prompts, the system structures the project, creates milestones, and can be used to enable small contractor businesses by providing automated workflows and commercialization paths ([00:57:21](#00:57:21)).

* **Use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for Structure and Competitive Advantage**: David Olsson discussed the value of finding existing structures in the world that parallel current work, noting that LLMs can learn from these external examples to add structure to their output. This process is beneficial from a competitive standpoint and helps in understanding complex processes, such as the evolution of a sales channel. David Olsson plans to share a document built around a parallel path to commercialization for a fermentation project, which involves using questions to understand necessary preconditions for capabilities ([00:58:51](#00:58:51)).

* **Project State Initialization and Management**: David Olsson described the "Project State" concept, which is designed to be industry-specific and capable of handling complex reasoning, such as isolating concerns and building context from multiple documents ([01:00:00](#01:00:00)). They explained that to initialize Project State, a user would navigate to a project folder, bring in necessary files, and then execute the "project state initialize" command, which would build the state within that folder ([01:00:54](#01:00:54)). The system is built to be somewhat invisible to the user by being set up as a "dot folder," but its function needs to be clearly communicated to knowledge workers ([01:00:00](#01:00:00)).

* **Real-time System Remediation and Tracking**: The Project State system is intended to be remedial and will track changes, affecting the system in real time by giving a new orientation, milestones, and risk assessments. If a decision is made to change the direction of an MVP, the system can grind through a significant number of tokens to retool the whole system and establish a new commercialization pattern ([01:01:53](#01:01:53)). David Olsson mentioned that inputs can be mid-streamed, meaning the system is designed to be changed while being used ([01:02:51](#01:02:51)).

* **Project State Harvesters and Organizational Intelligence**: Project State includes a series of harvesters that allow it to gather context, such as pulling relevant conversations from a person's email about a specific thread, which can then reorient the entire project schema ([01:02:51](#01:02:51)). David Olsson noted their first effort involves enabling people to work within the cloud ecosystem, with plans to potentially integrate with the Google and OpenAI command line interfaces ([01:03:58](#01:03:58)). The ultimate goal is to create a substrate of organizational intelligence by keeping the system simple and allowing for "naive prompting" once the structure is built ([01:05:07](#01:05:07)).

* **Upcoming White Paper and Business Structure**: David Olsson discussed the clarity they have regarding using these tools to solve business problems and create a base of organizational intelligence ([01:05:59](#01:05:59)). They noted that organizational structure is trending towards "fewer rooms, stronger walls," meaning fewer areas but more solid foundations for development KPIs ([01:06:49](#01:06:49)). David Olsson plans to circulate a white paper for review among Hanif Janmohamed, Eric, and others, intending to submit it to Arvix as intellectual property ([01:07:57](#01:07:57)).

*You should review Gemini's notes to make sure they're accurate. [Get tips and learn how Gemini takes notes](https://support.google.com/meet/answer/14754931)*
